title: Social and demographic
summary: 9609 A Level — demographics, lifestyle trends, ethics, and social change affecting markets.
learningObjectives: Analyse demographic trends on demand and labour.
Explain social and cultural influences on consumption.
Apply social PESTLE to marketing and HRM.

simpleExplanation.title: How society is changing
simpleExplanation.summary: Population structure, lifestyles, values, and attitudes shape what customers buy and how employees expect to work.
steps: Ageing population increases demand for healthcare, retirement services.
Urbanisation shifts retail and delivery patterns.
Work-life balance affects recruitment and remote work.

flashcards.back: Healthcare, pharmaceuticals, retirement housing, financial planning.
Higher density → delivery services, compact products, public transport markets.
Buyers prefer fair trade, sustainable sourcing — affects product and promotion.
Links to segmentation — age, income, family structure (3.1.6).
Changing attitudes can obsolete products (e.g. smoking decline).
Stakeholders expect responsible behaviour — reputational risk if ignored.

sections.content: **Social and demographic** (6.1.3) links **PESTLE to marketing** — trend analysis should lead to **segment/product** implications, not vague "society is changing".

sections.question: Country has rising vegan population (+40% in 5 years) and strong animal welfare activism. Impact on a traditional dairy company?
sections.solution:
**Threat:** **Falling demand** for milk; **negative publicity** from activists; retailers delist non-sustainable suppliers.

**Opportunity:** Launch **plant-based line**; **certify** welfare standards; **rebrand** as diversified "nutrition" firm.

**Marketing:** Target **flexitarian segment** (3.1.6); **promotion** emphasises sustainability (3.3.5); may need **premium pricing** for R&D costs.
